subject: Statistics
title: Choosing a Prior
description: A short lesson to help you apply Choosing a Prior.

hook.text: You've never seen this coin before. Is it fair? Your answer before flipping is your prior.

buildup.text: Priors come from past data, expert knowledge, or mathematical convenience. Each choice shapes the result.

discovery.text: An informative prior says 'I know something.' A vague prior says 'I know nothing.' Both are valid starting points.

twist.text: Priors are subjective by definition. Two Bayesians can start differently and converge as data arrives.

climax.text: With enough data, the prior washes out. The evidence speaks louder than the starting belief.

punchline.text: Every analysis starts somewhere. Bayesians are honest about where.

quiz.question: What happens to the influence of the prior as more data is collected?
options: It diminishes — with enough data, the evidence overwhelms the prior
It becomes stronger and harder to override
It stays constant regardless of data
